mnemonics: Punctuation Police: Java's separators are like grammar cops, ensuring code statements are properly structured and separated.
keyPoints: Separators are special symbols that define the structure and organization of code.

Common Java separators include: semicolon (;) ends statements, braces ({}) define code blocks, brackets ([]) for arrays, parentheses (()) for parameters/expressions, comma (,) separates items, and period (.) accesses members.
Unlike operators, separators don't perform actions but define how code is grouped and executed.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the semicolon (;) at the end of statements, causing syntax errors.
Mismatching braces ({}) or parentheses (()), which breaks code block structure.
Using commas instead of periods for member access (e.g., object,method() instead of object.method()).
